body: User requested faster workflow automation via Windows notification/event signals for DAN handoff and Claude session-limit awareness. Codex updated the existing Codex app heartbeat trinity-claude-signal-watch to run every 5 minutes, added _MODEL_TRINITY/WINDOWS_SIGNAL_WATCH_PROTOCOL.md, indexed it in _MODEL_TRINITY/README.md, added it to _MODEL_TRINITY/CLAUDE_NEXT_SESSION_HANDOFF_2026-06-14.md, and logged the config change. Immediate probe scripts/trinity_windows_signal_probe.py returned signals=[dan_handoff_hint] with privacy invariants raw_notification_text_read=false, credential_files_read=false, app_config_mutated=false, service_started=false. Bridge status outboxes were all 0 and rejected 0; check_trinity_secret_refs returned raw_secret_scan=pass. Claude should treat Windows Push Notification Platform data as metadata hints only and should not scrape raw notification bodies, mutate app config, install plugins/MCP, read credentials, start services/sockets, call providers, spend, deploy, or perform service writes without exact scoped approval.
constraints: metadata-only Windows signal watching;bridge-first handoff loop;no raw notification body scraping;no new live authority
forbidden_actions: raw notification body scraping;credential reads;app config mutation;MCP/plugin installs;service/socket starts;provider calls;service writes;public deploy;spending
objective: Notify Claude of accelerated Windows signal watch protocol
requested_output: Claude should read _MODEL_TRINITY/WINDOWS_SIGNAL_WATCH_PROTOCOL.md and audit whether its own watcher/wake workflow can follow the same metadata-only rules.
summary: Codex updated the Trinity Windows signal watch protocol and accelerated the Codex heartbeat trinity-claude-signal-watch to every 5 minutes. Claude may use the same metadata-only boundary for DAN handoff and session-limit signals.
